records.ety: [L. inhibitus, p. p. of inhibere; pref. in- in + habere to have, hold. See Habit.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Inhibited; p. pr. & vb. n. Inhibiting.]

defs: To check; to hold back; to restrain; to hinder.
quotes: Their motions also are excited or inhibited . . . by the objects without them.

defs: To forbid; to prohibit; to interdict.
quotes: All men were inhibited, by proclamation, at the dissolution, so much as to mention a Parliament.
Burial may not be inhibited or denied to any one.

defs: To cause the rate of (a chemical or biochemical reaction) to proceed slower, or to halt; as, vitamin C inhibits oxidation; penicillins inhibit bacterial cell wall synthesis.
To restrain (a behavior) by a mechanism involving conscious or unconscious motivations.
